message.content: why usb 3 fails but wsb 2 doesny

content.text:
Good question — it comes down to **current draw and a marginal power path.** Here's the mechanism, top to bottom.

## Short version
USB 3 makes the camera **pull more peak current**. That current flows through the USB cable + the hub connector, which have real resistance — so `voltage_drop = current × resistance`. On USB 3 the draw is high enough that the voltage **at the camera** sags below what its onboard 5 V regulator needs → the MyriadX browns out and resets → device drops to bootloader. On USB 2 the current is capped low enough that the voltage stays above that brownout point. Same cable, same camera — just less current.

This is why every drop is an **abrupt whole-device USB disconnect** with the `__watchdog` thread itself dying (power lost, not a comms timeout), and why **`get_throttled` never shows under-voltage** — the sag is *after* the cable, on the camera's local rail; the Pi's own 5 V rail upstream never dips enough to trip its detector.

## Why USB 3 draws more — three things stack up
1. **Power-class negotiation.** A USB 2 bus-powered device is spec-limited to **500 mA**; a USB 3 SuperSpeed device may negotiate up to **900 mA**. On USB 3 the OAK is *allowed* to pull more — and does, including bigger transient spikes.
2. **The SuperSpeed PHY itself.** The 5 Gbps SerDes transceiver burns meaningfully more power than the USB 2 high-speed PHY — a continuous extra draw just to run the link, before any imaging work.
3. **Full-bandwidth pipeline.** On USB 3 the heavy config actually *pushes* RGB 1080p@30 + depth at full rate, so the ISP/encoder/stereo engines run hard → high dynamic current with spikes. On USB 2 the ~27 MB/s bandwidth ceiling throttles the streams to ~5–6 fps, so those engines idle most of the time → much lower, smoother current.

## Why it's *heavy* + USB 3 specifically (not light + USB 3)
The matrix showed **light (depth-only) is stable on USB 3 too.** Depth-only at 6 fps draws little even with the SuperSpeed PHY overhead — total current stays under the brownout threshold. It's only when you add the **heavy RGB pipeline at full USB-3 bandwidth** that peak current crosses the edge. So it's not "USB 3 is broken" — it's "heavy pipeline + USB 3 = the one combination whose current exceeds what this power path can deliver."

## The key implication
This is a **power-delivery limit, not a USB-3 incompatibility.** Give the camera clean 5 V that *doesn't* come through the marginal cable/hub — a **powered USB hub** or the **Luxonis Y-adapter** — and USB 3 + heavy would very likely run fine at full framerate. Until then, the free workaround is exactly what the matrix says: stay on USB 2, or run depth-only (which is stable on either bus and all that mapping needs).

One honest caveat: I've pinned this to "current across the cable/connector resistance" from the *signature* (brownout, no Pi under-voltage, draw-dependent), but I can't isolate *which* link is marginal — cable vs connector vs the shared hub — without swapping hardware. A short/thick cable test or a powered hub would confirm it (and probably fix it).

message.content: okay, now update the project docs, dont overcomplicate docs keep it on point evidence based for future references

content.text: On it — I'll tighten `docs/camera.md` into a crisp, evidence-based reference and trim the narrative. Let me read the current version first so I preserve the accurate device facts while cutting the bloat:

content.content:
1	# OAK-D-LITE Camera
2	
3	The robot's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**), how it fails, how to recover, and
4	how it was fixed. Deep-dive on robot 468 (`turtlebot-van-468`, Raspberry Pi 4) — bench
5	test 2026-06-19, **root-cause investigation 2026-06-20**.
6	
7	> **2026-06-20 correction.** The earlier version of this doc blamed the camera's
8	> boot-loop/wedge on **power starvation** and prescribed forcing USB 2 + an eventual
9	> hardware Y-adapter. **That root cause was wrong.** Controlled experiments (below) show
10	> the real cause is the kernel's tiny default **`usbfs_memory_mb=16`** USB buffer, and a
11	> second, independent **Pi-4 CPU-saturation** failure at runtime. Across the *entire*
12	> investigation the Pi under-voltage flag **never latched** (`vcgencmd get_throttled`
13	> stayed `0x0`; the only flag ever seen, `0x80000`, was a *thermal* flag from a synthetic
14	> CPU stress test). Both real causes are fixed in software — see
15	> [`scripts/`](../scripts/README.md).
16	
17	## TL;DR
18	- The camera **works on USB 3 at full rate** once `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb` is raised from
19	  the default **16** to **256**. With the default 16, the depthai driver reaches
20	  "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** with `X_LINK_ERROR` on the stereo stream —
21	  this is the daily "boot loop / dead camera". One kernel param fixes it.
22	- A **second** failure hits at runtime: when the Pi-4 CPU saturates (SLAM + Nav2 + fusion;
23	  the depthai `camera_node` alone eats ~100 % of one core), the device's XLink keep-alive
24	  is missed, the device reboots to its bootloader, and the stock driver **sits wedged
25	  forever** (process alive, 0 frames). Fixed by `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`
26	  (driver self-heal) **plus** the [`oak_watchdog`](../scripts/README.md) backstop.
27	- It is **not** power, **not** the cable, **not** USB-3-incompatibility. Forcing USB 2 only
28	  ever *delayed* the failure (less bandwidth → the 16 MB buffer lasts longer) — which is
29	  why USB 2 "felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.
30	
31	---
32	
33	## What the camera is
34	
35	A **Luxonis OAK-D-LITE** (MXID `18443010B112591200`) on USB, providing **both** an RGB
36	stream and a stereo **depth** stream. There is **no separate USB webcam**; the `/dev/video*`
37	nodes are its internal codec/ISP devices (no `/dev/video0`). It is **not** UVC — it speaks
38	XLink over USB, driven through ROS 2.
39	
40	| Item | Value |
41	|---|---|
42	| Device | OAK-D-LITE, MXID `18443010B112591200` |
43	| USB ID (bootloader / booted) | `03e7:2485` (ROM bootloader) / `03e7:f63b` (firmware running) |
44	| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` (`ros-humble-depthai` **2.29.0**) |
45	| On-robot node / topics | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw`, `/oakd/imu/data`, … |
46	| Max USB speed | **SUPER** (USB 3 / 5 Gbps). `SUPER_PLUS` is requested by the TB4 config but the LITE caps at SUPER. |
47	| USB topology | behind a VIA Labs hub it **shares with the RPLIDAR**; bus-powered |
48	| Python `depthai` | **not** installed — use the ROS driver |
49	
50	The MyriadX firmware runs from **RAM**: any reset/disconnect drops the device back to its
51	ROM **bootloader (`2485`)**. So `2485` = "no driver is holding it" (idle *or* crashed);
52	`f63b` = "firmware booted and streaming". The watchdog and every health check key off this.
53	
54	### The id flips between buses
55	On reset the device re-enumerates as either USB 3 SuperSpeed (`Bus 02`, `5000M`) or USB 2
56	high-speed (`Bus 01`, `480M` behind the VIA hub) depending on what it negotiates. Both are
57	normal; what matters is `f63b` vs `2485`.
58	
59	---
60	
61	## How to bring it up (the good config)
62	
63	Use [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md) — it bakes in every fix:
64	
65	```bash
66	scripts/oak_bringup.sh                       # depth-only, USB2, hardened (mapping default)
67	OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER scripts/oak_bringup.sh   # full-rate USB3 (usbfs=256 makes it stable)
68	```
69	
70	A healthy start logs `USB SPEED: SUPER` (or `HIGH`), `Device type: OAK-D-LITE`, and
71	`Camera ready!` with **no** `X_LINK_ERROR` and no `Aborted`.
72	
73	### QoS / measurement gotcha (important)
74	- The driver publishes the depth image with **`RELIABLE`** QoS on the robot build (an
75	  earlier doc said BEST_EFFORT — wrong).
76	- More important: on this box's **FastDDS Discovery-Server + super-client** setup, a
77	  freshly-spawned ad-hoc subscriber (`ros2 topic echo/hz`, a one-off rclpy node) receives
78	  **zero** data — *for any topic*, including the lidar `/scan` (verified 2026-06-20). It is
79	  a discovery/transport artifact, not a dead publisher. **Do not health-check the camera by
80	  frame-probing from a throwaway node.** Use the **USB product-id** (`f63b` vs `2485`) plus
81	  `camera_node` process liveness — which is exactly what `oak_watchdog.sh` does. Also note
82	  `ros2 topic hz` in this build does **not** accept `--qos-reliability`.
83	
84	---
85	
86	## Failure taxonomy (what actually happens)
87	
88	| # | Mode | Signature | Cause | Fix |
89	|---|---|---|---|---|
90	| **F1** | **Bring-up abort** (the daily one) | reaches `Camera ready`, then ~5 s later `Couldn't read data from stream: 'stereo_stereo' (X_LINK_ERROR)` → `[ros2run]: Aborted`. Device falls to `2485`. | `usbfs_memory_mb=16` too small for the OAK's USB bulk transfers | **`usbfs_memory_mb=256`** |
91	| **F2** | **Runtime disconnect under load** | streams fine, then under Pi-4 CPU saturation the device drops to `2485`; driver logs `No data on logger queue! … 'sys_logger_queue' (X_LINK_ERROR)` every second | host misses the XLink keep-alive (the Pi can't service USB in time); `camera_node` itself burns ~100 % of a core | reduce CPU load (C++ fusion, combined runner) + `DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000` + self-heal + watchdog |
92	| **F3** | **Silent wedge / "container gave up"** | `camera_node`/`oakd_container` process **alive** but device stuck at `2485`, **0 frames, forever**, no recovery | F1/F2 that the stock driver never recovers from (it has no USB-disconnect callback; `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` defaults **false**). The TB4 `oakd_container` has **no `respawn`**, so a crash also leaves this state. | `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` + `oak_watchdog` |
93	| **F4** | **Frame drops (USB2 only)** | streams but ~5–6 fps of 30; no errors | USB 2 bandwidth ceiling (~27 MB/s vs ~135 MB/s for both streams @30) | run USB 3 (works now with usbfs=256), or lower fps/res |
94	| **F5** | MyriadX thermal shutdown | `Temperature above 105C` → disconnect; clears after cooldown | VPU hot on a fanless robot | not observed here (peaked 72 °C); heatsink if it appears |
95	
96	**The discriminator:** device back at **`2485`** = a *reset/reboot* (F1/F2/F3). Device stays
97	`f63b` but 0 Hz = pure XLink wedge. Process alive + `2485` + no activity = F3 (gave up).
98	
99	### The daily failure path, exactly
100	`turtlebot4.service` → `/tmp/turtlebot4.launch.py` → `oakd_container` (a plain
101	`ComposableNodeContainer`, **no respawn**) loads
102	`/opt/ros/humble/share/turtlebot4_bringup/config/oakd_lite.yaml` with `i_usb_speed:
103	SUPER_PLUS`, `i_enable_imu: true`, IR on, RGB 1080p@30. On a box with `usbfs=16` that hits
104	**F1** within seconds, the container dies, nothing respawns it → **F3**. The lone surviving
105	topic is `/turtlebot468/imu` (a dead advertisement — 0 msgs). That is the state the robot
106	was found in (device dead for ~76 min, process alive).
107	
108	---
109	
110	## Root cause — the evidence
111	
112	Controlled experiments on robot 468, 2026-06-20. Only **one variable** changed between
113	EXP1 and EXP2:
114	
115	| EXP | Config | `usbfs` | Result |
116	|---|---|---|---|
117	| **1** | TB4 default `oakd_lite.yaml` (SUPER/USB3) | **16** | `Camera ready` → **`X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → Aborted in ~5 s**; device → `2485` |
118	| **2/3** | **same config, unchanged** | **256** | `Camera ready` → **199 s+ continuous on USB3 SuperSpeed, 0 disconnects**, `throttled=0x0`, temp ≤ 72.5 °C |
119	| **5** | full RGBD + 3-core CPU load | 256 | streamed, then dropped to `2485` under load; driver **wedged forever** (F3), `throttled=0x0` |
120	| **6** | + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` | 256 | driver **self-healed** the wedge (rebooted device, `Camera ready` again) — but **thrashes** (restart-loops) if the trigger persists, because the driver has no backoff |
121	
122	Key points the evidence nails down:
123	- **Power is not the cause.** `get_throttled` stayed `0x0` through every bring-up crash and
124	  every runtime drop. The only flag ever seen (`0x80000`, soft-temp) came from a synthetic
125	  3-core stress test, not the camera. The earlier "`0x50000` proves power" reading was a
126	  one-off and is not necessary or sufficient for the failure.
127	- **USB 3 was never the problem** — the *exact* `SUPER` config that crashed with usbfs=16
128	  runs indefinitely with usbfs=256. Forcing USB 2 only lowered bandwidth so the 16 MB
129	  buffer lasted longer (minutes instead of seconds) — hence "USB 2 felt stable" but still
130	  dropped.
131	- **The runtime disconnect is *current draw* — not CPU, not temperature** *(corrected by the
132	  2026-06-20 mode-matrix + load-sweep below; an earlier draft blamed CPU saturation, but that
133	  came from loading the **heavy** config, which brownouts regardless of load — a confound).*
134	  The depthai `camera_node` does eat ~100 % of one Pi-4 core, which throttles framerate/SLAM —
135	  but it does **not** drop the camera (see the load sweep).
136	
137	## Mode matrix — what works / what doesn't
138	
139	Each mode soaked 5 min with auto-recover-and-retry (multiple trials → a reliable drop *rate*,
140	not one stochastic sample). All with `usbfs=256`:
141	
142	| Config | USB2 (HIGH) | USB3 (SUPER) |
143	|---|---|---|
144	| **depth-only** (light: no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
145	| **RGB + depth + IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ **~30 s MTTD** (2 of 3 trials) |
146	
147	**Exactly one mode fails: heavy-pipeline + USB3** — the peak-current combination (USB3 pulls
148	the 900 mA power class *and* runs RGB at full bandwidth). Drop either USB3 or the RGB pipeline
149	and it is stable.
150	
151	## Power vs CPU vs temperature — verdict: **power (draw)**
152	
153	Load sweep on the good mode (depth-only + USB2) under increasing CPU load, 3 min/level:
154	
155	| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC temp | VPU temp | Throttle |
156	|---|---|---|---|---|
157	| 0 cores | 0 | 73.5 °C | 44.9 °C | — |
158	| 1 core | 0 | 80.3 °C | 44.9 °C | — |
159	| 2 cores | 0 | 81.3 °C | 45.5 °C | — |
160	| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi **actively thermal-throttling** |
161	
162	- **CPU — ruled out.** Depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops.
163	- **Temperature — ruled out.** The VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed at **45 °C**; the
164	  Pi SoC hit 82.7 °C and throttled its *own* CPU, yet the camera never dropped. The
165	  under-voltage bit never set in any test.
166	- **Power (draw) — confirmed.** The only failing mode is heavy+USB3, at idle, independent of
167	  CPU/temperature. The drop signature (depthai debug) is an abrupt whole-device USB
168	  disconnect in which the `__watchdog` thread itself dies trying to write — i.e. a momentary
169	  brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail (USB cable/connector IR-drop under peak draw),
170	  invisible to the Pi's under-voltage detector.
171	- **Real-stack confirmation:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (producing
172	  `scan_fused` + `/map`, no motion) held steady on `f63b` under the real mapping load — so
173	  **running the full mapping stack alongside the depth-only camera is safe.**
174	
175	**The true fix for the residual heavy+USB3 brownout is hardware** (power delivery, not
176	software): a shorter/thicker USB cable, a powered USB hub, or the Luxonis Y-adapter. Software
177	mitigations: run the **light depth-only config** (mapping doesn't need RGB) and let the
178	watchdog/self-heal recover the rare drop.
179	
180	## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
181	- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` runs on `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry — no IMU
182	  (`slam_fused.yaml` has no IMU params). The OAK IMU stream is unused; keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
183	  The Create3 base has its own IMU if anything ever needs one.
184	- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only; RGB is unused for mapping.
185	  RGB+depth *can* run together (heavy+USB2 is stable) but at reduced framerate — so depth-only
186	  is both lighter and the recommended mapping mode.
187	
188	---
189	
190	## Recovery playbook (no physical replug)
191	
192	All validated on-robot 2026-06-20. Resolve the OAK by **vendor `03e7`** every time (the id
193	flips `2485`↔`f63b`). **Never `pkill -f camera_node`** — that matches your own shell command
194	(CLAUDE.md gotcha #6); use `pkill -x camera_node`.
195	
196	```bash
197	# 1. Plain relaunch — recovers F1/F3 from the 2485 state in ~9 s (REQUIRES usbfs>=256)
198	pkill -x camera_node
199	scripts/oak_bringup.sh
200	
201	# 2. usbreset escalation — when relaunch alone won't take (this build's usbreset wants vid:pid)
202	pkill -x camera_node
203	sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # e.g. 03e7:2485 or 03e7:f63b
204	scripts/oak_bringup.sh
205	
206	# 3. deeper escalations (rarely needed): software replug, then driver rebind
207	P=$(for d in /sys/bus/usb/devices/*; do grep -q 03e7 "$d/idVendor" 2>/dev/null && echo "$d" && break; done)
208	echo 0 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"; echo 1 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"      # software unplug/replug
209	ID=$(basename "$P"); echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/unbind; echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/bind
210	# (uhubctl true power-cycle is NOT available — not installed, and Pi-4 USB-A ports are ganged)
211	```
212	
213	Or just let **`oak_watchdog.sh`** do all of this automatically (it was validated recovering
214	a killed camera end-to-end: detect → 30 s grace → kill → usbreset → relaunch → healthy).
215	
216	---
217	
218	## Prevention — apply once, never wedge again
219	
220	Ranked, all software (no hardware needed). Installer: `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`.
221	
222	1. **`usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256`** (+ `usbcore.autosuspend=-1`) in
223	   `/boot/firmware/cmdline.txt` → kills F1 permanently, enables stable USB 3. *The single
224	   most important fix.* (Set at runtime already; cmdline makes it survive reboot.)
225	2. **`i_enable_diagnostics:=true` + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`** → driver
226	   self-heals F2/F3 instead of wedging forever.
227	3. **`DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000`** (+ `_INITIAL_DELAY=12000`) → more keep-alive slack before
228	   the device gives up on a momentarily CPU-starved host.
229	4. **Cut Pi-4 CPU load** so F2 stops being triggered: the C++ fusion port
230	   (`guide_mate_perception`), the combined `guide_mate_bringup` runner, and killing unused
231	   nodes (joy/teleop/diagnostics). This is the real cure for the runtime drop.
232	5. **`oak_watchdog.service`** → external backstop (USB-id health, grace + backoff) for the
233	   residual cases the driver can't self-heal (dead process, hard wedge).
234	
235	Things that **don't** help: `max_usb_current=1` (no-op on Pi 4); a Y-adapter / powered hub
236	(would fix a power problem we don't have — only worth it if F5 thermal or a genuine
237	under-voltage ever shows up, neither of which was observed).
238	
239	---
240	
241	## Quick reference — diagnostics
242	
243	```bash
244	lsusb -d 03e7:                              # f63b=streaming, 2485=bootloader, empty=gone
245	cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # MUST be >=256
246	vcgencmd get_throttled                      # 0x0 = no power/thermal event (it stays 0x0 here)
247	vcgencmd measure_temp                       # SoC temp
248	sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE 'idProduct=(f63b|2485)|disconnect'   # USB enumerate/disconnect history
249	journalctl -u oak-watchdog -f               # watchdog activity (once enabled)
250	```
251

file.content:
# OAK-D-LITE Camera

The robot's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**), how it fails, how to recover, and
how it was fixed. Deep-dive on robot 468 (`turtlebot-van-468`, Raspberry Pi 4) — bench
test 2026-06-19, **root-cause investigation 2026-06-20**.

> **2026-06-20 correction.** The earlier version of this doc blamed the camera's
> boot-loop/wedge on **power starvation** and prescribed forcing USB 2 + an eventual
> hardware Y-adapter. **That root cause was wrong.** Controlled experiments (below) show
> the real cause is the kernel's tiny default **`usbfs_memory_mb=16`** USB buffer, and a
> second, independent **Pi-4 CPU-saturation** failure at runtime. Across the *entire*
> investigation the Pi under-voltage flag **never latched** (`vcgencmd get_throttled`
> stayed `0x0`; the only flag ever seen, `0x80000`, was a *thermal* flag from a synthetic
> CPU stress test). Both real causes are fixed in software — see
> [`scripts/`](../scripts/README.md).

## TL;DR
- The camera **works on USB 3 at full rate** once `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb` is raised from
  the default **16** to **256**. With the default 16, the depthai driver reaches
  "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** with `X_LINK_ERROR` on the stereo stream —
  this is the daily "boot loop / dead camera". One kernel param fixes it.
- A **second** failure hits at runtime: when the Pi-4 CPU saturates (SLAM + Nav2 + fusion;
  the depthai `camera_node` alone eats ~100 % of one core), the device's XLink keep-alive
  is missed, the device reboots to its bootloader, and the stock driver **sits wedged
  forever** (process alive, 0 frames). Fixed by `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`
  (driver self-heal) **plus** the [`oak_watchdog`](../scripts/README.md) backstop.
- It is **not** power, **not** the cable, **not** USB-3-incompatibility. Forcing USB 2 only
  ever *delayed* the failure (less bandwidth → the 16 MB buffer lasts longer) — which is
  why USB 2 "felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.

---

## What the camera is

A **Luxonis OAK-D-LITE** (MXID `18443010B112591200`) on USB, providing **both** an RGB
stream and a stereo **depth** stream. There is **no separate USB webcam**; the `/dev/video*`
nodes are its internal codec/ISP devices (no `/dev/video0`). It is **not** UVC — it speaks
XLink over USB, driven through ROS 2.

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Device | OAK-D-LITE, MXID `18443010B112591200` |
| USB ID (bootloader / booted) | `03e7:2485` (ROM bootloader) / `03e7:f63b` (firmware running) |
| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` (`ros-humble-depthai` **2.29.0**) |
| On-robot node / topics | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw`, `/oakd/imu/data`, … |
| Max USB speed | **SUPER** (USB 3 / 5 Gbps). `SUPER_PLUS` is requested by the TB4 config but the LITE caps at SUPER. |
| USB topology | behind a VIA Labs hub it **shares with the RPLIDAR**; bus-powered |
| Python `depthai` | **not** installed — use the ROS driver |

The MyriadX firmware runs from **RAM**: any reset/disconnect drops the device back to its
ROM **bootloader (`2485`)**. So `2485` = "no driver is holding it" (idle *or* crashed);
`f63b` = "firmware booted and streaming". The watchdog and every health check key off this.

### The id flips between buses
On reset the device re-enumerates as either USB 3 SuperSpeed (`Bus 02`, `5000M`) or USB 2
high-speed (`Bus 01`, `480M` behind the VIA hub) depending on what it negotiates. Both are
normal; what matters is `f63b` vs `2485`.

---

## How to bring it up (the good config)

Use [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md) — it bakes in every fix:

```bash
scripts/oak_bringup.sh                       # depth-only, USB2, hardened (mapping default)
OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER scripts/oak_bringup.sh   # full-rate USB3 (usbfs=256 makes it stable)
```

A healthy start logs `USB SPEED: SUPER` (or `HIGH`), `Device type: OAK-D-LITE`, and
`Camera ready!` with **no** `X_LINK_ERROR` and no `Aborted`.

### QoS / measurement gotcha (important)
- The driver publishes the depth image with **`RELIABLE`** QoS on the robot build (an
  earlier doc said BEST_EFFORT — wrong).
- More important: on this box's **FastDDS Discovery-Server + super-client** setup, a
  freshly-spawned ad-hoc subscriber (`ros2 topic echo/hz`, a one-off rclpy node) receives
  **zero** data — *for any topic*, including the lidar `/scan` (verified 2026-06-20). It is
  a discovery/transport artifact, not a dead publisher. **Do not health-check the camera by
  frame-probing from a throwaway node.** Use the **USB product-id** (`f63b` vs `2485`) plus
  `camera_node` process liveness — which is exactly what `oak_watchdog.sh` does. Also note
  `ros2 topic hz` in this build does **not** accept `--qos-reliability`.

---

## Failure taxonomy (what actually happens)

| # | Mode | Signature | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **F1** | **Bring-up abort** (the daily one) | reaches `Camera ready`, then ~5 s later `Couldn't read data from stream: 'stereo_stereo' (X_LINK_ERROR)` → `[ros2run]: Aborted`. Device falls to `2485`. | `usbfs_memory_mb=16` too small for the OAK's USB bulk transfers | **`usbfs_memory_mb=256`** |
| **F2** | **Runtime disconnect under load** | streams fine, then under Pi-4 CPU saturation the device drops to `2485`; driver logs `No data on logger queue! … 'sys_logger_queue' (X_LINK_ERROR)` every second | host misses the XLink keep-alive (the Pi can't service USB in time); `camera_node` itself burns ~100 % of a core | reduce CPU load (C++ fusion, combined runner) + `DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000` + self-heal + watchdog |
| **F3** | **Silent wedge / "container gave up"** | `camera_node`/`oakd_container` process **alive** but device stuck at `2485`, **0 frames, forever**, no recovery | F1/F2 that the stock driver never recovers from (it has no USB-disconnect callback; `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` defaults **false**). The TB4 `oakd_container` has **no `respawn`**, so a crash also leaves this state. | `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` + `oak_watchdog` |
| **F4** | **Frame drops (USB2 only)** | streams but ~5–6 fps of 30; no errors | USB 2 bandwidth ceiling (~27 MB/s vs ~135 MB/s for both streams @30) | run USB 3 (works now with usbfs=256), or lower fps/res |
| **F5** | MyriadX thermal shutdown | `Temperature above 105C` → disconnect; clears after cooldown | VPU hot on a fanless robot | not observed here (peaked 72 °C); heatsink if it appears |

**The discriminator:** device back at **`2485`** = a *reset/reboot* (F1/F2/F3). Device stays
`f63b` but 0 Hz = pure XLink wedge. Process alive + `2485` + no activity = F3 (gave up).

### The daily failure path, exactly
`turtlebot4.service` → `/tmp/turtlebot4.launch.py` → `oakd_container` (a plain
`ComposableNodeContainer`, **no respawn**) loads
`/opt/ros/humble/share/turtlebot4_bringup/config/oakd_lite.yaml` with `i_usb_speed:
SUPER_PLUS`, `i_enable_imu: true`, IR on, RGB 1080p@30. On a box with `usbfs=16` that hits
**F1** within seconds, the container dies, nothing respawns it → **F3**. The lone surviving
topic is `/turtlebot468/imu` (a dead advertisement — 0 msgs). That is the state the robot
was found in (device dead for ~76 min, process alive).

---

## Root cause — the evidence

Controlled experiments on robot 468, 2026-06-20. Only **one variable** changed between
EXP1 and EXP2:

| EXP | Config | `usbfs` | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | TB4 default `oakd_lite.yaml` (SUPER/USB3) | **16** | `Camera ready` → **`X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → Aborted in ~5 s**; device → `2485` |
| **2/3** | **same config, unchanged** | **256** | `Camera ready` → **199 s+ continuous on USB3 SuperSpeed, 0 disconnects**, `throttled=0x0`, temp ≤ 72.5 °C |
| **5** | full RGBD + 3-core CPU load | 256 | streamed, then dropped to `2485` under load; driver **wedged forever** (F3), `throttled=0x0` |
| **6** | + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` | 256 | driver **self-healed** the wedge (rebooted device, `Camera ready` again) — but **thrashes** (restart-loops) if the trigger persists, because the driver has no backoff |

Key points the evidence nails down:
- **Power is not the cause.** `get_throttled` stayed `0x0` through every bring-up crash and
  every runtime drop. The only flag ever seen (`0x80000`, soft-temp) came from a synthetic
  3-core stress test, not the camera. The earlier "`0x50000` proves power" reading was a
  one-off and is not necessary or sufficient for the failure.
- **USB 3 was never the problem** — the *exact* `SUPER` config that crashed with usbfs=16
  runs indefinitely with usbfs=256. Forcing USB 2 only lowered bandwidth so the 16 MB
  buffer lasted longer (minutes instead of seconds) — hence "USB 2 felt stable" but still
  dropped.
- **The runtime disconnect is *current draw* — not CPU, not temperature** *(corrected by the
  2026-06-20 mode-matrix + load-sweep below; an earlier draft blamed CPU saturation, but that
  came from loading the **heavy** config, which brownouts regardless of load — a confound).*
  The depthai `camera_node` does eat ~100 % of one Pi-4 core, which throttles framerate/SLAM —
  but it does **not** drop the camera (see the load sweep).

## Mode matrix — what works / what doesn't

Each mode soaked 5 min with auto-recover-and-retry (multiple trials → a reliable drop *rate*,
not one stochastic sample). All with `usbfs=256`:

| Config | USB2 (HIGH) | USB3 (SUPER) |
|---|---|---|
| **depth-only** (light: no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
| **RGB + depth + IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ **~30 s MTTD** (2 of 3 trials) |

**Exactly one mode fails: heavy-pipeline + USB3** — the peak-current combination (USB3 pulls
the 900 mA power class *and* runs RGB at full bandwidth). Drop either USB3 or the RGB pipeline
and it is stable.

## Power vs CPU vs temperature — verdict: **power (draw)**

Load sweep on the good mode (depth-only + USB2) under increasing CPU load, 3 min/level:

| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC temp | VPU temp | Throttle |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 cores | 0 | 73.5 °C | 44.9 °C | — |
| 1 core | 0 | 80.3 °C | 44.9 °C | — |
| 2 cores | 0 | 81.3 °C | 45.5 °C | — |
| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi **actively thermal-throttling** |

- **CPU — ruled out.** Depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops.
- **Temperature — ruled out.** The VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed at **45 °C**; the
  Pi SoC hit 82.7 °C and throttled its *own* CPU, yet the camera never dropped. The
  under-voltage bit never set in any test.
- **Power (draw) — confirmed.** The only failing mode is heavy+USB3, at idle, independent of
  CPU/temperature. The drop signature (depthai debug) is an abrupt whole-device USB
  disconnect in which the `__watchdog` thread itself dies trying to write — i.e. a momentary
  brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail (USB cable/connector IR-drop under peak draw),
  invisible to the Pi's under-voltage detector.
- **Real-stack confirmation:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (producing
  `scan_fused` + `/map`, no motion) held steady on `f63b` under the real mapping load — so
  **running the full mapping stack alongside the depth-only camera is safe.**

**The true fix for the residual heavy+USB3 brownout is hardware** (power delivery, not
software): a shorter/thicker USB cable, a powered USB hub, or the Luxonis Y-adapter. Software
mitigations: run the **light depth-only config** (mapping doesn't need RGB) and let the
watchdog/self-heal recover the rare drop.

## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` runs on `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry — no IMU
  (`slam_fused.yaml` has no IMU params). The OAK IMU stream is unused; keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
  The Create3 base has its own IMU if anything ever needs one.
- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only; RGB is unused for mapping.
  RGB+depth *can* run together (heavy+USB2 is stable) but at reduced framerate — so depth-only
  is both lighter and the recommended mapping mode.

---

## Recovery playbook (no physical replug)

All validated on-robot 2026-06-20. Resolve the OAK by **vendor `03e7`** every time (the id
flips `2485`↔`f63b`). **Never `pkill -f camera_node`** — that matches your own shell command
(CLAUDE.md gotcha #6); use `pkill -x camera_node`.

```bash
# 1. Plain relaunch — recovers F1/F3 from the 2485 state in ~9 s (REQUIRES usbfs>=256)
pkill -x camera_node
scripts/oak_bringup.sh

# 2. usbreset escalation — when relaunch alone won't take (this build's usbreset wants vid:pid)
pkill -x camera_node
sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # e.g. 03e7:2485 or 03e7:f63b
scripts/oak_bringup.sh

# 3. deeper escalations (rarely needed): software replug, then driver rebind
P=$(for d in /sys/bus/usb/devices/*; do grep -q 03e7 "$d/idVendor" 2>/dev/null && echo "$d" && break; done)
echo 0 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"; echo 1 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"      # software unplug/replug
ID=$(basename "$P"); echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/unbind; echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/bind
# (uhubctl true power-cycle is NOT available — not installed, and Pi-4 USB-A ports are ganged)
```

Or just let **`oak_watchdog.sh`** do all of this automatically (it was validated recovering
a killed camera end-to-end: detect → 30 s grace → kill → usbreset → relaunch → healthy).

---

## Prevention — apply once, never wedge again

Ranked, all software (no hardware needed). Installer: `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`.

1. **`usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256`** (+ `usbcore.autosuspend=-1`) in
   `/boot/firmware/cmdline.txt` → kills F1 permanently, enables stable USB 3. *The single
   most important fix.* (Set at runtime already; cmdline makes it survive reboot.)
2. **`i_enable_diagnostics:=true` + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`** → driver
   self-heals F2/F3 instead of wedging forever.
3. **`DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000`** (+ `_INITIAL_DELAY=12000`) → more keep-alive slack before
   the device gives up on a momentarily CPU-starved host.
4. **Cut Pi-4 CPU load** so F2 stops being triggered: the C++ fusion port
   (`guide_mate_perception`), the combined `guide_mate_bringup` runner, and killing unused
   nodes (joy/teleop/diagnostics). This is the real cure for the runtime drop.
5. **`oak_watchdog.service`** → external backstop (USB-id health, grace + backoff) for the
   residual cases the driver can't self-heal (dead process, hard wedge).

Things that **don't** help: `max_usb_current=1` (no-op on Pi 4); a Y-adapter / powered hub
(would fix a power problem we don't have — only worth it if F5 thermal or a genuine
under-voltage ever shows up, neither of which was observed).

---

## Quick reference — diagnostics

```bash
lsusb -d 03e7:                              # f63b=streaming, 2485=bootloader, empty=gone
cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # MUST be >=256
vcgencmd get_throttled                      # 0x0 = no power/thermal event (it stays 0x0 here)
vcgencmd measure_temp                       # SoC temp
sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE 'idProduct=(f63b|2485)|disconnect'   # USB enumerate/disconnect history
journalctl -u oak-watchdog -f               # watchdog activity (once enabled)
```

aiTitle: Investigate camera and depth sensor issues

content.text: The doc has accumulated contradictions from my iterative edits — the early sections still carry the wrong "CPU saturation" framing while the later ones have the corrected "power/draw" finding, and there's a now-false "a Y-adapter wouldn't help" line. That's exactly the bloat to fix. Rewriting it tight, internally consistent, evidence-first:

input.content:
# OAK-D-LITE Camera

Robot 468's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**) — how it fails, how to recover, how it's
fixed. From the root-cause investigation on `turtlebot-van-468` (Pi 4, ROS 2 Humble), 2026-06-20.

## TL;DR
Two distinct failures, both now understood. *(Both an earlier "power starvation → force USB2 →
need a Y-adapter" write-up and a later "CPU saturation" draft were wrong; the evidence below
supersedes them.)*

1. **Daily wedge** — camera reaches "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** (`X_LINK_ERROR` on
   the stereo stream); the TB4 container has no respawn, so it sits dead at bootloader.
   **Cause: kernel `usbfs_memory_mb=16` is too small for the OAK's USB transfers. Fix: set it to
   256** (one line, permanent via `cmdline.txt`).
2. **Residual intermittent drop** — *only* the **heavy RGB+depth pipeline on USB3** drops (~30 s);
   everything lighter is stable. **Cause: a current-draw brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail**
   (USB cable/connector IR-drop under USB3 peak draw) — **not** CPU, **not** temperature, **not**
   Pi under-voltage (that flag never set in any test). Avoid by running depth-only / USB2; the
   true fix is better power delivery (hardware).

**Stable operating config = depth-only, USB2, `usbfs=256`** → [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md).
It survives a fully-saturated, thermally-throttling Pi without dropping.

## What the camera is
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Device | OAK-D-LITE, MXID `18443010B112591200` |
| USB id | `03e7:2485` = ROM bootloader (no driver / crashed) · `03e7:f63b` = firmware running (streaming) |
| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` 2.29.0 (no standalone python `depthai`) |
| Node / depth topic | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw` |
| USB | bus-powered, behind a VIA hub **shared with the RPLIDAR**; max speed SUPER (USB3, 5 Gbps) |

Firmware runs from RAM, so any reset drops the device to bootloader (`2485`). **Health = USB
product-id (`f63b` vs `2485`) + `camera_node` alive** — *not* frame-probing: on this FastDDS
Discovery-Server box a freshly-spawned `ros2 topic echo/hz` subscriber gets 0 frames for *any*
topic (even `/scan`) — a transport artifact, not a dead publisher. (`ros2 topic hz` here also
rejects `--qos-reliability`; the depth publisher QoS is `RELIABLE`.)

## What works / what doesn't (mode matrix)
5 min/mode, auto-recover-and-retry (reliable drop *rate*, not one sample), all with `usbfs=256`:

| Config | USB2 (HIGH) | USB3 (SUPER) |
|---|---|---|
| **depth-only** (no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
| **RGB+depth+IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ ~30 s mean-time-to-drop |

→ **Exactly one mode fails: heavy + USB3.**

## Why USB3 fails but USB2 doesn't
USB3 makes the camera draw **more peak current** — it negotiates the 900 mA power class (vs
500 mA on USB2), the SuperSpeed PHY itself burns more, and the heavy pipeline runs RGB at full
bandwidth. That current, across the cable/connector resistance, sags the voltage *at the camera*
below its regulator's dropout → the MyriadX resets. USB2 caps the current low enough to stay
above it. Depth-only draws little, so it's under the threshold on either bus.

## Power vs CPU vs temperature → **power (draw)**
Load sweep, depth-only + USB2, 3 min/level:

| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC | VPU | Throttle |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 / 1 / 2 cores | 0 | 73 → 81 °C | ~45 °C | — |
| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi thermal-throttling |

- **CPU — ruled out:** depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops. (The
  `camera_node` ~100 %-of-a-core cost hurts framerate, not stability.)
- **Temperature — ruled out:** the VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed 45 °C; the Pi SoC
  got hot and throttled its *own* CPU, but never dropped the camera.
- **Power (draw) — confirmed:** only heavy+USB3 drops, at idle, load-independent. Signature =
  abrupt whole-device USB disconnect (the depthai `__watchdog` thread itself dies) → a rail
  brownout, not a comms timeout.
- **Real-stack check:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (`scan_fused` + `/map`
  live, no motion) held steady → **the full mapping stack alongside depth-only is safe.**

## Root-cause evidence (the usbfs fix)
Single-variable flip, same TB4-default config:

| `usbfs_memory_mb` | Result |
|---|---|
| **16** (kernel default) | "Camera ready" → `X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → **aborts in ~5 s** → device `2485` |
| **256** | "Camera ready" → **200 s+ continuous, 0 disconnects** (`throttled=0x0`) |

Only `usbfs` changed. Forcing USB2 only *delayed* the crash (lower bandwidth drains the 16 MB
buffer slower) — which is why "USB2 felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.

## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` uses `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry (no IMU params in
  `slam_fused.yaml`). The Create3 has its own IMU if ever needed. Keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only. RGB+depth *can* run together
  (stable on USB2) but mapping doesn't need RGB → depth-only is lighter and recommended.

## Bring-up, recovery, prevention
- **Bring-up:** `scripts/oak_bringup.sh` (depth-only / USB2 / `usbfs` ensured; `OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER`
  for USB3). Healthy start = `Camera ready!`, no `X_LINK_ERROR`, no `Aborted`.
- **Recover a wedge** — never `pkill -f camera_node` (matches your own shell → exit 144); use `-x`:
  ```bash
  pkill -x camera_node                                   # device → 2485
  sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # if a plain relaunch won't take (vid:pid form)
  scripts/oak_bringup.sh                                  # recovers from 2485 in ~9 s (needs usbfs>=256)
  ```
  Or let `scripts/oak_watchdog.sh` do it automatically (USB-id health, 30 s grace, kill→usbreset→
  relaunch, with backoff — validated end-to-end).
- **Prevent permanently** — `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`, then reboot:
  1. `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256` (+ `autosuspend=-1`) in `cmdline.txt` — **the** fix for the daily wedge.
  2. `i_enable_diagnostics:=true -p i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` — driver self-heals a drop (no backoff → pair with the watchdog).
  3. `oak_watchdog.service` — external backstop.
  4. For the heavy+USB3 brownout specifically: **hardware power** (short/thick cable, powered USB hub, or Luxonis Y-adapter) — or just run depth-only.

## Quick diagnostics
```bash
lsusb -d 03e7:                                       # f63b=streaming · 2485=bootloader · empty=gone
cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # must be >=256
vcgencmd get_throttled                               # under-voltage bit (0x1/0x10000) never sets here
sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE '03e7|disconnect'           # USB enumerate/disconnect history
```

# OAK-D-LITE Camera

Robot 468's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**) — how it fails, how to recover, how it's
fixed. From the root-cause investigation on `turtlebot-van-468` (Pi 4, ROS 2 Humble), 2026-06-20.

## TL;DR
Two distinct failures, both now understood. *(Both an earlier "power starvation → force USB2 →
need a Y-adapter" write-up and a later "CPU saturation" draft were wrong; the evidence below
supersedes them.)*

1. **Daily wedge** — camera reaches "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** (`X_LINK_ERROR` on
   the stereo stream); the TB4 container has no respawn, so it sits dead at bootloader.
   **Cause: kernel `usbfs_memory_mb=16` is too small for the OAK's USB transfers. Fix: set it to
   256** (one line, permanent via `cmdline.txt`).
2. **Residual intermittent drop** — *only* the **heavy RGB+depth pipeline on USB3** drops (~30 s);
   everything lighter is stable. **Cause: a current-draw brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail**
   (USB cable/connector IR-drop under USB3 peak draw) — **not** CPU, **not** temperature, **not**
   Pi under-voltage (that flag never set in any test). Avoid by running depth-only / USB2; the
   true fix is better power delivery (hardware).

**Stable operating config = depth-only, USB2, `usbfs=256`** → [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md).
It survives a fully-saturated, thermally-throttling Pi without dropping.

## What the camera is
| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Device | OAK-D-LITE, MXID `18443010B112591200` |
| USB id | `03e7:2485` = ROM bootloader (no driver / crashed) · `03e7:f63b` = firmware running (streaming) |
| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` 2.29.0 (no standalone python `depthai`) |
| Node / depth topic | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw` |
| USB | bus-powered, behind a VIA hub **shared with the RPLIDAR**; max speed SUPER (USB3, 5 Gbps) |

Firmware runs from RAM, so any reset drops the device to bootloader (`2485`). **Health = USB
product-id (`f63b` vs `2485`) + `camera_node` alive** — *not* frame-probing: on this FastDDS
Discovery-Server box a freshly-spawned `ros2 topic echo/hz` subscriber gets 0 frames for *any*
topic (even `/scan`) — a transport artifact, not a dead publisher. (`ros2 topic hz` here also
rejects `--qos-reliability`; the depth publisher QoS is `RELIABLE`.)

## What works / what doesn't (mode matrix)
5 min/mode, auto-recover-and-retry (reliable drop *rate*, not one sample), all with `usbfs=256`:

| Config | USB2 (HIGH) | USB3 (SUPER) |
|---|---|---|
| **depth-only** (no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
| **RGB+depth+IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ ~30 s mean-time-to-drop |

→ **Exactly one mode fails: heavy + USB3.**

## Why USB3 fails but USB2 doesn't
USB3 makes the camera draw **more peak current** — it negotiates the 900 mA power class (vs
500 mA on USB2), the SuperSpeed PHY itself burns more, and the heavy pipeline runs RGB at full
bandwidth. That current, across the cable/connector resistance, sags the voltage *at the camera*
below its regulator's dropout → the MyriadX resets. USB2 caps the current low enough to stay
above it. Depth-only draws little, so it's under the threshold on either bus.

## Power vs CPU vs temperature → **power (draw)**
Load sweep, depth-only + USB2, 3 min/level:

| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC | VPU | Throttle |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 / 1 / 2 cores | 0 | 73 → 81 °C | ~45 °C | — |
| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi thermal-throttling |

- **CPU — ruled out:** depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops. (The
  `camera_node` ~100 %-of-a-core cost hurts framerate, not stability.)
- **Temperature — ruled out:** the VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed 45 °C; the Pi SoC
  got hot and throttled its *own* CPU, but never dropped the camera.
- **Power (draw) — confirmed:** only heavy+USB3 drops, at idle, load-independent. Signature =
  abrupt whole-device USB disconnect (the depthai `__watchdog` thread itself dies) → a rail
  brownout, not a comms timeout.
- **Real-stack check:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (`scan_fused` + `/map`
  live, no motion) held steady → **the full mapping stack alongside depth-only is safe.**

## Root-cause evidence (the usbfs fix)
Single-variable flip, same TB4-default config:

| `usbfs_memory_mb` | Result |
|---|---|
| **16** (kernel default) | "Camera ready" → `X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → **aborts in ~5 s** → device `2485` |
| **256** | "Camera ready" → **200 s+ continuous, 0 disconnects** (`throttled=0x0`) |

Only `usbfs` changed. Forcing USB2 only *delayed* the crash (lower bandwidth drains the 16 MB
buffer slower) — which is why "USB2 felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.

## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` uses `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry (no IMU params in
  `slam_fused.yaml`). The Create3 has its own IMU if ever needed. Keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only. RGB+depth *can* run together
  (stable on USB2) but mapping doesn't need RGB → depth-only is lighter and recommended.

## Bring-up, recovery, prevention
- **Bring-up:** `scripts/oak_bringup.sh` (depth-only / USB2 / `usbfs` ensured; `OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER`
  for USB3). Healthy start = `Camera ready!`, no `X_LINK_ERROR`, no `Aborted`.
- **Recover a wedge** — never `pkill -f camera_node` (matches your own shell → exit 144); use `-x`:
  ```bash
  pkill -x camera_node                                   # device → 2485
  sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # if a plain relaunch won't take (vid:pid form)
  scripts/oak_bringup.sh                                  # recovers from 2485 in ~9 s (needs usbfs>=256)
  ```
  Or let `scripts/oak_watchdog.sh` do it automatically (USB-id health, 30 s grace, kill→usbreset→
  relaunch, with backoff — validated end-to-end).
- **Prevent permanently** — `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`, then reboot:
  1. `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256` (+ `autosuspend=-1`) in `cmdline.txt` — **the** fix for the daily wedge.
  2. `i_enable_diagnostics:=true -p i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` — driver self-heals a drop (no backoff → pair with the watchdog).
  3. `oak_watchdog.service` — external backstop.
  4. For the heavy+USB3 brownout specifically: **hardware power** (short/thick cable, powered USB hub, or Luxonis Y-adapter) — or just run depth-only.

## Quick diagnostics
```bash
lsusb -d 03e7:                                       # f63b=streaming · 2485=bootloader · empty=gone
cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # must be >=256
vcgencmd get_throttled                               # under-voltage bit (0x1/0x10000) never sets here
sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE '03e7|disconnect'           # USB enumerate/disconnect history
```

lines: # OAK-D-LITE Camera
-The robot's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**), how it fails, how to recover, and
-how it was fixed. Deep-dive on robot 468 (`turtlebot-van-468`, Raspberry Pi 4) — bench
-test 2026-06-19, **root-cause investigation 2026-06-20**.
+Robot 468's depth/RGB camera (Luxonis **OAK-D-LITE**) — how it fails, how to recover, how it's
+fixed. From the root-cause investigation on `turtlebot-van-468` (Pi 4, ROS 2 Humble), 2026-06-20.
-> **2026-06-20 correction.** The earlier version of this doc blamed the camera's
-> boot-loop/wedge on **power starvation** and prescribed forcing USB 2 + an eventual
-> hardware Y-adapter. **That root cause was wrong.** Controlled experiments (below) show
-> the real cause is the kernel's tiny default **`usbfs_memory_mb=16`** USB buffer, and a
-> second, independent **Pi-4 CPU-saturation** failure at runtime. Across the *entire*
-> investigation the Pi under-voltage flag **never latched** (`vcgencmd get_throttled`
-> stayed `0x0`; the only flag ever seen, `0x80000`, was a *thermal* flag from a synthetic
-> CPU stress test). Both real causes are fixed in software — see
-> [`scripts/`](../scripts/README.md).
## TL;DR
-- The camera **works on USB 3 at full rate** once `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb` is raised from
-  the default **16** to **256**. With the default 16, the depthai driver reaches
-  "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** with `X_LINK_ERROR` on the stereo stream —
-  this is the daily "boot loop / dead camera". One kernel param fixes it.
-- A **second** failure hits at runtime: when the Pi-4 CPU saturates (SLAM + Nav2 + fusion;
-  the depthai `camera_node` alone eats ~100 % of one core), the device's XLink keep-alive
-  is missed, the device reboots to its bootloader, and the stock driver **sits wedged
-  forever** (process alive, 0 frames). Fixed by `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`
-  (driver self-heal) **plus** the [`oak_watchdog`](../scripts/README.md) backstop.
-- It is **not** power, **not** the cable, **not** USB-3-incompatibility. Forcing USB 2 only
-  ever *delayed* the failure (less bandwidth → the 16 MB buffer lasts longer) — which is
-  why USB 2 "felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.
+Two distinct failures, both now understood. *(Both an earlier "power starvation → force USB2 →
+need a Y-adapter" write-up and a later "CPU saturation" draft were wrong; the evidence below
+supersedes them.)*
+1. **Daily wedge** — camera reaches "Camera ready" then **aborts ~5 s later** (`X_LINK_ERROR` on
+   the stereo stream); the TB4 container has no respawn, so it sits dead at bootloader.
+   **Cause: kernel `usbfs_memory_mb=16` is too small for the OAK's USB transfers. Fix: set it to
+   256** (one line, permanent via `cmdline.txt`).
+2. **Residual intermittent drop** — *only* the **heavy RGB+depth pipeline on USB3** drops (~30 s);
+   everything lighter is stable. **Cause: a current-draw brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail**
+   (USB cable/connector IR-drop under USB3 peak draw) — **not** CPU, **not** temperature, **not**
+   Pi under-voltage (that flag never set in any test). Avoid by running depth-only / USB2; the
+   true fix is better power delivery (hardware).
-## What the camera is
+**Stable operating config = depth-only, USB2, `usbfs=256`** → [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md).
+It survives a fully-saturated, thermally-throttling Pi without dropping.
-A **Luxonis OAK-D-LITE** (MXID `18443010B112591200`) on USB, providing **both** an RGB
-stream and a stereo **depth** stream. There is **no separate USB webcam**; the `/dev/video*`
-nodes are its internal codec/ISP devices (no `/dev/video0`). It is **not** UVC — it speaks
-XLink over USB, driven through ROS 2.
+## What the camera is
| Item | Value |
| Device | OAK-D-LITE, MXID `18443010B112591200` |
-| USB ID (bootloader / booted) | `03e7:2485` (ROM bootloader) / `03e7:f63b` (firmware running) |
-| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` (`ros-humble-depthai` **2.29.0**) |
-| On-robot node / topics | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw`, `/oakd/imu/data`, … |
-| Max USB speed | **SUPER** (USB 3 / 5 Gbps). `SUPER_PLUS` is requested by the TB4 config but the LITE caps at SUPER. |
-| USB topology | behind a VIA Labs hub it **shares with the RPLIDAR**; bus-powered |
-| Python `depthai` | **not** installed — use the ROS driver |
+| USB id | `03e7:2485` = ROM bootloader (no driver / crashed) · `03e7:f63b` = firmware running (streaming) |
+| Driver | `depthai_ros_driver` 2.29.0 (no standalone python `depthai`) |
+| Node / depth topic | `/turtlebot468/oakd` → `/turtlebot468/oakd/stereo/image_raw` |
+| USB | bus-powered, behind a VIA hub **shared with the RPLIDAR**; max speed SUPER (USB3, 5 Gbps) |
-The MyriadX firmware runs from **RAM**: any reset/disconnect drops the device back to its
-ROM **bootloader (`2485`)**. So `2485` = "no driver is holding it" (idle *or* crashed);
-`f63b` = "firmware booted and streaming". The watchdog and every health check key off this.
+Firmware runs from RAM, so any reset drops the device to bootloader (`2485`). **Health = USB
+product-id (`f63b` vs `2485`) + `camera_node` alive** — *not* frame-probing: on this FastDDS
+Discovery-Server box a freshly-spawned `ros2 topic echo/hz` subscriber gets 0 frames for *any*
+topic (even `/scan`) — a transport artifact, not a dead publisher. (`ros2 topic hz` here also
+rejects `--qos-reliability`; the depth publisher QoS is `RELIABLE`.)
-### The id flips between buses
-On reset the device re-enumerates as either USB 3 SuperSpeed (`Bus 02`, `5000M`) or USB 2
-high-speed (`Bus 01`, `480M` behind the VIA hub) depending on what it negotiates. Both are
-normal; what matters is `f63b` vs `2485`.
+## What works / what doesn't (mode matrix)
+5 min/mode, auto-recover-and-retry (reliable drop *rate*, not one sample), all with `usbfs=256`:
-## How to bring it up (the good config)
-Use [`scripts/oak_bringup.sh`](../scripts/README.md) — it bakes in every fix:
-```bash
-scripts/oak_bringup.sh                       # depth-only, USB2, hardened (mapping default)
-OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER scripts/oak_bringup.sh   # full-rate USB3 (usbfs=256 makes it stable)
-A healthy start logs `USB SPEED: SUPER` (or `HIGH`), `Device type: OAK-D-LITE`, and
-`Camera ready!` with **no** `X_LINK_ERROR` and no `Aborted`.
-### QoS / measurement gotcha (important)
-- The driver publishes the depth image with **`RELIABLE`** QoS on the robot build (an
-  earlier doc said BEST_EFFORT — wrong).
-- More important: on this box's **FastDDS Discovery-Server + super-client** setup, a
-  freshly-spawned ad-hoc subscriber (`ros2 topic echo/hz`, a one-off rclpy node) receives
-  **zero** data — *for any topic*, including the lidar `/scan` (verified 2026-06-20). It is
-  a discovery/transport artifact, not a dead publisher. **Do not health-check the camera by
-  frame-probing from a throwaway node.** Use the **USB product-id** (`f63b` vs `2485`) plus
-  `camera_node` process liveness — which is exactly what `oak_watchdog.sh` does. Also note
-  `ros2 topic hz` in this build does **not** accept `--qos-reliability`.
-## Failure taxonomy (what actually happens)
-| # | Mode | Signature | Cause | Fix |

-| **F1** | **Bring-up abort** (the daily one) | reaches `Camera ready`, then ~5 s later `Couldn't read data from stream: 'stereo_stereo' (X_LINK_ERROR)` → `[ros2run]: Aborted`. Device falls to `2485`. | `usbfs_memory_mb=16` too small for the OAK's USB bulk transfers | **`usbfs_memory_mb=256`** |

-| **F2** | **Runtime disconnect under load** | streams fine, then under Pi-4 CPU saturation the device drops to `2485`; driver logs `No data on logger queue! … 'sys_logger_queue' (X_LINK_ERROR)` every second | host misses the XLink keep-alive (the Pi can't service USB in time); `camera_node` itself burns ~100 % of a core | reduce CPU load (C++ fusion, combined runner) + `DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000` + self-heal + watchdog |

-| **F3** | **Silent wedge / "container gave up"** | `camera_node`/`oakd_container` process **alive** but device stuck at `2485`, **0 frames, forever**, no recovery | F1/F2 that the stock driver never recovers from (it has no USB-disconnect callback; `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` defaults **false**). The TB4 `oakd_container` has **no `respawn`**, so a crash also leaves this state. | `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` + `oak_watchdog` |

-| **F4** | **Frame drops (USB2 only)** | streams but ~5–6 fps of 30; no errors | USB 2 bandwidth ceiling (~27 MB/s vs ~135 MB/s for both streams @30) | run USB 3 (works now with usbfs=256), or lower fps/res |
-| **F5** | MyriadX thermal shutdown | `Temperature above 105C` → disconnect; clears after cooldown | VPU hot on a fanless robot | not observed here (peaked 72 °C); heatsink if it appears |
-**The discriminator:** device back at **`2485`** = a *reset/reboot* (F1/F2/F3). Device stays
-`f63b` but 0 Hz = pure XLink wedge. Process alive + `2485` + no activity = F3 (gave up).
-### The daily failure path, exactly
-`turtlebot4.service` → `/tmp/turtlebot4.launch.py` → `oakd_container` (a plain
-`ComposableNodeContainer`, **no respawn**) loads
-`/opt/ros/humble/share/turtlebot4_bringup/config/oakd_lite.yaml` with `i_usb_speed:
-SUPER_PLUS`, `i_enable_imu: true`, IR on, RGB 1080p@30. On a box with `usbfs=16` that hits
-**F1** within seconds, the container dies, nothing respawns it → **F3**. The lone surviving
-topic is `/turtlebot468/imu` (a dead advertisement — 0 msgs). That is the state the robot
-was found in (device dead for ~76 min, process alive).
-## Root cause — the evidence
-Controlled experiments on robot 468, 2026-06-20. Only **one variable** changed between
-EXP1 and EXP2:
-| EXP | Config | `usbfs` | Result |
-| **1** | TB4 default `oakd_lite.yaml` (SUPER/USB3) | **16** | `Camera ready` → **`X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → Aborted in ~5 s**; device → `2485` |
-| **2/3** | **same config, unchanged** | **256** | `Camera ready` → **199 s+ continuous on USB3 SuperSpeed, 0 disconnects**, `throttled=0x0`, temp ≤ 72.5 °C |
-| **5** | full RGBD + 3-core CPU load | 256 | streamed, then dropped to `2485` under load; driver **wedged forever** (F3), `throttled=0x0` |

-| **6** | + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` | 256 | driver **self-healed** the wedge (rebooted device, `Camera ready` again) — but **thrashes** (restart-loops) if the trigger persists, because the driver has no backoff |
-Key points the evidence nails down:
-- **Power is not the cause.** `get_throttled` stayed `0x0` through every bring-up crash and
-  every runtime drop. The only flag ever seen (`0x80000`, soft-temp) came from a synthetic
-  3-core stress test, not the camera. The earlier "`0x50000` proves power" reading was a
-  one-off and is not necessary or sufficient for the failure.
-- **USB 3 was never the problem** — the *exact* `SUPER` config that crashed with usbfs=16
-  runs indefinitely with usbfs=256. Forcing USB 2 only lowered bandwidth so the 16 MB
-  buffer lasted longer (minutes instead of seconds) — hence "USB 2 felt stable" but still
-  dropped.
-- **The runtime disconnect is *current draw* — not CPU, not temperature** *(corrected by the
-  2026-06-20 mode-matrix + load-sweep below; an earlier draft blamed CPU saturation, but that
-  came from loading the **heavy** config, which brownouts regardless of load — a confound).*
-  The depthai `camera_node` does eat ~100 % of one Pi-4 core, which throttles framerate/SLAM —
-  but it does **not** drop the camera (see the load sweep).
-## Mode matrix — what works / what doesn't
-Each mode soaked 5 min with auto-recover-and-retry (multiple trials → a reliable drop *rate*,
-not one stochastic sample). All with `usbfs=256`:
| Config | USB2 (HIGH) | USB3 (SUPER) |
-| **depth-only** (light: no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
-| **RGB + depth + IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ **~30 s MTTD** (2 of 3 trials) |
+| **depth-only** (no RGB, no IMU, 6 fps) | ✅ 0 drops / 287 s | ✅ 0 drops / 286 s |
+| **RGB+depth+IMU** (heavy = TB4 default) | ✅ 0 drops / 284 s | ❌ ~30 s mean-time-to-drop |
-**Exactly one mode fails: heavy-pipeline + USB3** — the peak-current combination (USB3 pulls
-the 900 mA power class *and* runs RGB at full bandwidth). Drop either USB3 or the RGB pipeline
-and it is stable.
+→ **Exactly one mode fails: heavy + USB3.**
-## Power vs CPU vs temperature — verdict: **power (draw)**
+## Why USB3 fails but USB2 doesn't
+USB3 makes the camera draw **more peak current** — it negotiates the 900 mA power class (vs
+500 mA on USB2), the SuperSpeed PHY itself burns more, and the heavy pipeline runs RGB at full
+bandwidth. That current, across the cable/connector resistance, sags the voltage *at the camera*
+below its regulator's dropout → the MyriadX resets. USB2 caps the current low enough to stay
+above it. Depth-only draws little, so it's under the threshold on either bus.
-Load sweep on the good mode (depth-only + USB2) under increasing CPU load, 3 min/level:
+## Power vs CPU vs temperature → **power (draw)**
+Load sweep, depth-only + USB2, 3 min/level:
-| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC temp | VPU temp | Throttle |
+| Extra CPU load | Drops | SoC | VPU | Throttle |
-| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi **actively thermal-throttling** |
+| 3 cores (Pi saturated) | 0 | 82.7 °C | 45.6 °C | `0x80008` = Pi thermal-throttling |
-- **CPU — ruled out.** Depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops.
-- **Temperature — ruled out.** The VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed at **45 °C**; the
-  Pi SoC hit 82.7 °C and throttled its *own* CPU, yet the camera never dropped. The
-  under-voltage bit never set in any test.
-- **Power (draw) — confirmed.** The only failing mode is heavy+USB3, at idle, independent of
-  CPU/temperature. The drop signature (depthai debug) is an abrupt whole-device USB
-  disconnect in which the `__watchdog` thread itself dies trying to write — i.e. a momentary
-  brownout on the camera's own 5 V rail (USB cable/connector IR-drop under peak draw),
-  invisible to the Pi's under-voltage detector.
-- **Real-stack confirmation:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (producing
-  `scan_fused` + `/map`, no motion) held steady on `f63b` under the real mapping load — so
-  **running the full mapping stack alongside the depth-only camera is safe.**
+- **CPU — ruled out:** depth-only survived full saturation (~3.85 of 4 cores), 0 drops. (The
+  `camera_node` ~100 %-of-a-core cost hurts framerate, not stability.)
+- **Temperature — ruled out:** the VPU (the chip that would overheat) stayed 45 °C; the Pi SoC
+  got hot and throttled its *own* CPU, but never dropped the camera.
+- **Power (draw) — confirmed:** only heavy+USB3 drops, at idle, load-independent. Signature =
+  abrupt whole-device USB disconnect (the depthai `__watchdog` thread itself dies) → a rail
+  brownout, not a comms timeout.
+- **Real-stack check:** camera + `depth_lidar_fusion` + `slam_toolbox` (`scan_fused` + `/map`
+  live, no motion) held steady → **the full mapping stack alongside depth-only is safe.**
-**The true fix for the residual heavy+USB3 brownout is hardware** (power delivery, not
-software): a shorter/thicker USB cable, a powered USB hub, or the Luxonis Y-adapter. Software
-mitigations: run the **light depth-only config** (mapping doesn't need RGB) and let the
-watchdog/self-heal recover the rare drop.
+## Root-cause evidence (the usbfs fix)
+Single-variable flip, same TB4-default config:
-## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
-- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` runs on `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry — no IMU
-  (`slam_fused.yaml` has no IMU params). The OAK IMU stream is unused; keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
-  The Create3 base has its own IMU if anything ever needs one.
-- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only; RGB is unused for mapping.
-  RGB+depth *can* run together (heavy+USB2 is stable) but at reduced framerate — so depth-only
-  is both lighter and the recommended mapping mode.
+| `usbfs_memory_mb` | Result |
+| **16** (kernel default) | "Camera ready" → `X_LINK_ERROR` on stereo → **aborts in ~5 s** → device `2485` |
+| **256** | "Camera ready" → **200 s+ continuous, 0 disconnects** (`throttled=0x0`) |
+Only `usbfs` changed. Forcing USB2 only *delayed* the crash (lower bandwidth drains the 16 MB
+buffer slower) — which is why "USB2 felt stable" but still dropped after minutes.
-## Recovery playbook (no physical replug)
+## Do we need RGB or the IMU? No.
+- **IMU:** `slam_toolbox` uses `scan_fused` + Create3 wheel odometry (no IMU params in
+  `slam_fused.yaml`). The Create3 has its own IMU if ever needed. Keep `i_enable_imu:=false`.
+- **RGB:** `depth_lidar_fusion` consumes the **depth** stream only. RGB+depth *can* run together
+  (stable on USB2) but mapping doesn't need RGB → depth-only is lighter and recommended.
-All validated on-robot 2026-06-20. Resolve the OAK by **vendor `03e7`** every time (the id
-flips `2485`↔`f63b`). **Never `pkill -f camera_node`** — that matches your own shell command
-(CLAUDE.md gotcha #6); use `pkill -x camera_node`.
+## Bring-up, recovery, prevention
+- **Bring-up:** `scripts/oak_bringup.sh` (depth-only / USB2 / `usbfs` ensured; `OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER`
+  for USB3). Healthy start = `Camera ready!`, no `X_LINK_ERROR`, no `Aborted`.
+- **Recover a wedge** — never `pkill -f camera_node` (matches your own shell → exit 144); use `-x`:
+  ```bash
+  pkill -x camera_node                                   # device → 2485
+  sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # if a plain relaunch won't take (vid:pid form)
+  scripts/oak_bringup.sh                                  # recovers from 2485 in ~9 s (needs usbfs>=256)
+  Or let `scripts/oak_watchdog.sh` do it automatically (USB-id health, 30 s grace, kill→usbreset→
+  relaunch, with backoff — validated end-to-end).
+- **Prevent permanently** — `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`, then reboot:
+  1. `usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256` (+ `autosuspend=-1`) in `cmdline.txt` — **the** fix for the daily wedge.
+  2. `i_enable_diagnostics:=true -p i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` — driver self-heals a drop (no backoff → pair with the watchdog).
+  3. `oak_watchdog.service` — external backstop.
+  4. For the heavy+USB3 brownout specifically: **hardware power** (short/thick cable, powered USB hub, or Luxonis Y-adapter) — or just run depth-only.
+## Quick diagnostics
```bash
-# 1. Plain relaunch — recovers F1/F3 from the 2485 state in ~9 s (REQUIRES usbfs>=256)
-pkill -x camera_node
-scripts/oak_bringup.sh
-# 2. usbreset escalation — when relaunch alone won't take (this build's usbreset wants vid:pid)
-pkill -x camera_node
-sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # e.g. 03e7:2485 or 03e7:f63b
-scripts/oak_bringup.sh
-# 3. deeper escalations (rarely needed): software replug, then driver rebind
-P=$(for d in /sys/bus/usb/devices/*; do grep -q 03e7 "$d/idVendor" 2>/dev/null && echo "$d" && break; done)
-echo 0 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"; echo 1 | sudo tee "$P/authorized"      # software unplug/replug
-ID=$(basename "$P"); echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/unbind; echo "$ID" | sudo tee /sys/bus/usb/drivers/usb/bind
-# (uhubctl true power-cycle is NOT available — not installed, and Pi-4 USB-A ports are ganged)
+lsusb -d 03e7:                                       # f63b=streaming · 2485=bootloader · empty=gone
+cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # must be >=256
+vcgencmd get_throttled                               # under-voltage bit (0x1/0x10000) never sets here
+sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE '03e7|disconnect'           # USB enumerate/disconnect history
-Or just let **`oak_watchdog.sh`** do all of this automatically (it was validated recovering
-a killed camera end-to-end: detect → 30 s grace → kill → usbreset → relaunch → healthy).
-## Prevention — apply once, never wedge again
-Ranked, all software (no hardware needed). Installer: `sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh`.
-1. **`usbcore.usbfs_memory_mb=256`** (+ `usbcore.autosuspend=-1`) in
-   `/boot/firmware/cmdline.txt` → kills F1 permanently, enables stable USB 3. *The single
-   most important fix.* (Set at runtime already; cmdline makes it survive reboot.)
-2. **`i_enable_diagnostics:=true` + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true`** → driver
-   self-heals F2/F3 instead of wedging forever.
-3. **`DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG=10000`** (+ `_INITIAL_DELAY=12000`) → more keep-alive slack before
-   the device gives up on a momentarily CPU-starved host.
-4. **Cut Pi-4 CPU load** so F2 stops being triggered: the C++ fusion port
-   (`guide_mate_perception`), the combined `guide_mate_bringup` runner, and killing unused
-   nodes (joy/teleop/diagnostics). This is the real cure for the runtime drop.
-5. **`oak_watchdog.service`** → external backstop (USB-id health, grace + backoff) for the
-   residual cases the driver can't self-heal (dead process, hard wedge).
-Things that **don't** help: `max_usb_current=1` (no-op on Pi 4); a Y-adapter / powered hub
-(would fix a power problem we don't have — only worth it if F5 thermal or a genuine
-under-voltage ever shows up, neither of which was observed).
-## Quick reference — diagnostics
-```bash
-lsusb -d 03e7:                              # f63b=streaming, 2485=bootloader, empty=gone
-cat /sys/module/usbcore/parameters/usbfs_memory_mb   # MUST be >=256
-vcgencmd get_throttled                      # 0x0 = no power/thermal event (it stays 0x0 here)
-vcgencmd measure_temp                       # SoC temp
-sudo dmesg -T | grep -iE 'idProduct=(f63b|2485)|disconnect'   # USB enumerate/disconnect history
-journalctl -u oak-watchdog -f               # watchdog activity (once enabled)

# OAK-D-LITE reliability scripts

Tooling that came out of the 2026-06-20 camera/depth deep-dive (full write-up:
[`docs/camera.md`](../docs/camera.md)). It fixes the daily camera wedge and supervises
the device so it can never silently stay dead.

## TL;DR — what was actually wrong
The camera's daily "boot loop / wedge" was **not** power starvation (the old docs were
wrong — under-voltage never once latched across the whole investigation). It is the
kernel default **`usbfs_memory_mb=16`**, which is too small for the OAK's USB bulk
transfers: the depthai driver throws `X_LINK_ERROR` reading the stereo stream and the
process aborts ~5 s after "Camera ready". Raising `usbfs_memory_mb` to 256 lets the
**exact same `SUPER`/USB3 config run indefinitely**. A second, independent failure hits
at runtime when the Pi-4 CPU saturates (SLAM+Nav2+fusion): the XLink keep-alive is
missed, the device reboots to its bootloader, and the driver — with stock params — sits
wedged forever. Two params fix that: `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error` (driver self-heal)
plus this watchdog (backstop).

## Files
| file | what it does |
|---|---|
| `oak_bringup.sh` | Hardened standalone camera bring-up. The single source of truth for the good config (usbfs ensured, `DEPTHAI_WATCHDOG`, diagnostics auto-restart, shallow queues). Depth-only/USB2 by default; `OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER` for full-rate USB3. |
| `oak_watchdog.sh` | Supervises the OAK by **USB product-id** (`f63b`=healthy, `2485`/absent=wedged) + process liveness — frame-probing is impossible on this Discovery-Server box. On a confirmed wedge: kill → `usbreset` → relaunch, with a 30 s grace (so it never fights the driver's own self-heal) and burst back-off (so it never thrashes). |
| `oak-watchdog.service` | systemd unit that runs `oak_watchdog.sh` with `Restart=always`. |
| `install_oak_fixes.sh` | `sudo`-run installer: persists `usbfs_memory_mb=256` + `usbcore.autosuspend=-1` in `cmdline.txt` (with backup), installs the sudoers rule and the watchdog unit. Does **not** reboot or auto-enable anything. |

## Quick use (no install)
```bash
# bring the camera up the hardened way (depth-only, USB2):
scripts/oak_bringup.sh
# ...or full-rate USB3 (needs usbfs>=256, which the script ensures):
OAK_USB_SPEED=SUPER scripts/oak_bringup.sh
```

## Permanent install
```bash
sudo bash scripts/install_oak_fixes.sh   # review it first
sudo reboot                              # locks in the cmdline usbfs/autosuspend change
```

## Deployment — pick ONE owner of the camera
The OAK is a single device; only one driver may hold it.
- **Recommended (mapping runs):** take the camera OUT of `turtlebot4.service` and let the
  watchdog own a standalone `oak_bringup.sh`:
  ```bash
  sudo systemctl enable --now oak-watchdog.service
  ```
- **Keep it in `turtlebot4.service`:** then just apply fix #1 (usbfs) and add
  `i_enable_diagnostics:=true` + `i_restart_on_diagnostics_error:=true` to
  `/opt/ros/humble/share/turtlebot4_bringup/config/oakd_lite.yaml`. Don't also enable the
  watchdog's standalone bring-up, or two drivers will fight for the device.

`touch /tmp/oak_watchdog.pause` suspends watchdog recovery during maintenance.

## Manual recovery (if you ever need it by hand)
```bash
pkill -x camera_node                       # kill the wedged driver (NEVER `pkill -f camera_node`)
sudo usbreset "$(lsusb -d 03e7: | awk '{print $6}')"   # reset whichever id is current (f63b/2485)
scripts/oak_bringup.sh                      # relaunch
```

-**exact same `SUPER`/USB3 config run indefinitely**. A second, independent failure hits

# cs7980 Guide-Mate — Documentation

Working documentation for the cs7980 TurtleBot 4 project.

## Index

### Network
- [Network overview](network/README.md) — how the robots are connected, addressing, discovery model
- [Connecting a TurtleBot 4 to NUwave](network/nuwave-connection.md) — step-by-step, including the 5 GHz fix that was the real blocker
- [ROS 2 over NUwave — what works and what doesn't](network/ros2-over-nuwave.md) — compatibility findings

### Camera
- [OAK-D-LITE camera — overview, test, findings, fixes, pending issues](camera.md) — the OAK-D-LITE depth/RGB camera; both streams work on USB 2; USB 3 boot-loop root-caused to power; bandwidth-limited frame drops

### Mapping & navigation
- [Autonomous mapping — overview](mapping/README.md) — BFS frontier explorer + SLAM + Nav2, glass handling, how to run, status
- [Depth camera for mapping](mapping/depth-perception.md) — using the OAK-D depth to see glass the lidar can't: FOV, the height-filtered pipeline, compute, and the planned lidar-scan injection
- [Viewing live mapping in RViz](mapping/rviz-visualization.md) — laptop-side RViz for the map, lidar, depth scan and cloud; the tf-remap, QoS, and Lite-vs-Standard model gotchas
- Code: [`src/guide_mate_explorer`](../src/guide_mate_explorer) (Python) — `bfs_explorer`, `glass_guard`, `depth_lidar_fusion`, plus a combined `guide_mate_bringup` runner (glass_guard + bfs_explorer in one process); [`src/guide_mate_perception`](../src/guide_mate_perception) (C++) — a faithful rclcpp port of `depth_lidar_fusion` (~10× cheaper on the Pi 4)

### Power & battery
- [Power consumption & saving](power.md) — battery-powered robot; only `battery_state` metering; ~14 W idle; measured draw, what's always running, and the working "park" (stop SLAM → lidar auto-idles + CPU freed); what does *not* work

### AWS IoT Core (cloud connectivity)
- [AWS IoT Core overview](aws-iot/README.md) — what we set up on robot 468, where it could help, and the security note on keeping credentials out of the repo
- [What IoT Core is — building blocks](aws-iot/iot-core-overview.md) — things, certs/policies, the MQTT broker, shadows, rules, jobs, Greengrass; how our files map to each
- [IoT Core ↔ other AWS services + EC2 access](aws-iot/service-integration.md) — the Rules Engine, service-to-device, EventBridge, and the three ways EC2 can reach IoT Core
- [Secure Tunneling — remote SSH to a robot](aws-iot/secure-tunneling.md) — source/destination tokens, `localproxy`, the MQTT auto-delivery path, and why it isn't connected yet

---

## ⚠️ Security note
**Do not commit credentials to this repo.** NUwave usernames/passwords, robot passwords, and any private keys must stay out of version control. The docs use placeholders such as `<nuwave-username>` and `<nuwave-password>`; keep the real values in a local, gitignored file or a password manager.

## Quick facts
- **Robots:** two TurtleBot 4 units — `turtlebot468` (room 468) and `turtlebot436` (room 436).
- **Each robot = two computers:** a Raspberry Pi 4 (Ubuntu 22.04, ROS 2 Humble — the thing you SSH into) and an iRobot Create 3 base (joined to the Pi by USB-C, which is both power and a wired Ethernet link).
- **Discovery model:** FastDDS **Discovery Server** running on the Pi; the Create 3 stays off WiFi and talks to the Pi over the USB-C link.
- **Default fallback network:** a local ASUS router, SSID `ASUS_98` (the robots auto-connect to it).

+- [OAK-D-LITE camera — root cause, mode matrix, fixes](camera.md) — daily wedge root-caused to `usbfs_memory_mb=16` (fix: 256, not power); residual drop is the heavy-RGBD+USB3 current brownout (not CPU/temp); depth-only/USB2 is stable; bring-up + watchdog in [`scripts/`](../scripts/README.md)
